authenticators.name: Behavioral features of rhythm input
authenticators.description: Behavioral biometric based on how a user performs a rhythm (timing dynamics and execution features) captured during input and matched to a template.

authenticators.name: Brain activity patterns
authenticators.description: Physiological biometric based on EEG-derived brain activity features captured during a task and matched to an enrolled template.

authenticators.name: Private Cryptographic Key
authenticators.description: A private asymmetric cryptographic key stored in software (e.g., a key file or browser credential store). Used to sign a challenge issued by the verifier; possession is proven without revealing the key.

authenticators.name: DRAM chip initialization patterns
authenticators.description: A physical unclonable function derived from DRAM power-up/initialization patterns, used as a device fingerprint for authentication.

authenticators.name: Facial features
authenticators.description: Physiological biometric based on facial feature descriptors extracted from an image and compared against a stored template.

authenticators.name: Fingerprint features
authenticators.description: A biometric authenticator based on the unique ridge and valley patterns of a person's fingertip. The fingerprint image is captured by a sensor and compared against a stored template using feature extraction algorithms.

authenticators.name: Gaze Password
authenticators.description: A knowledge factor entered via eye gaze; the user follows or fixates on targets in a specific sequence and timing to authenticate.

authenticators.name: Group signature certificate
authenticators.description: A digital certificate and associated group-signature credentials that allow a device to prove membership in a group anonymously.

authenticators.name: Hand micro-movement patterns
authenticators.description: Behavioral biometric based on involuntary micro-movements of the hand measured by sensors and matched as a dynamic signature.

authenticators.name: Hand vein pattern
authenticators.description: Physiological biometric based on the vascular pattern of the hand captured with near-infrared imaging and compared to an enrolled template.

authenticators.name: Handwriting features
authenticators.description: Behavioral biometric based on distinctive handwriting or air-writing motion characteristics captured as time-series features and matched to an enrolled template.

authenticators.name: Hardware OTP Token
authenticators.description: A dedicated physical device that generates one-time passwords using a built-in clock and shared secret. The generated code is entered by the user and verified server-side.

authenticators.name: Heartbeat patterns
authenticators.description: Physiological biometric based on cardiac signatures (e.g., radar/ECG-like temporal patterns) captured passively and matched to an enrolled profile.

authenticators.name: Initialization Key
authenticators.description: A digital secret (seed/shared key) used by a software OTP generator to derive one-time codes, proving possession of the initialized token state.

authenticators.name: Iris patterns
authenticators.description: Physiological biometric based on unique iris texture patterns extracted from an eye image and compared to a stored template.

authenticators.name: Keystroke Rhythm
authenticators.description: Behavioral biometric based on typing cadence (dwell/flight times and rhythms) captured during text entry and matched to a template.

authenticators.name: Knuckle shape
authenticators.description: Physiological biometric based on hand/knuckle geometry or surface features captured by an imaging sensor and matched to a template.

authenticators.name: Neuromuscular biometrics
authenticators.description: Physiological biometric based on neuromuscular signals (e.g., EMG) captured during a gesture or action and matched to an enrolled template.

authenticators.name: Photoplethysmogram (PPG) pulse patterns
authenticators.description: Physiological biometric based on PPG pulse waveform features captured by a wearable sensor and matched to an enrolled template.

authenticators.name: Physiological hand features
authenticators.description: Physiological biometric derived from measured hand characteristics (e.g., geometry/skin traits) captured during use and matched to a stored template.

authenticators.name: PIN
authenticators.description: A short numeric or alphanumeric code memorised by the user, typically 4–8 digits. Entered via a keypad or touch screen and compared against a stored value.

authenticators.name: Private cryptographic key with digital certificate
authenticators.description: An asymmetric private key used together with an associated digital certificate to authenticate by signing challenges and presenting certified public-key identity.

authenticators.name: Pulse Active Ratio (PRA) patterns
authenticators.description: Physiological biometric derived from pulse active ratio features (from ECG-like signals) captured passively and matched to an enrolled profile.

authenticators.name: RFID tag
authenticators.description: A physical token containing an RFID identifier/secret that responds to reader challenges, enabling possession-based authentication.

authenticators.name: Smart Card
authenticators.description: A physical card containing an embedded microprocessor and cryptographic key material. Authentication is performed by inserting the card into a reader, which challenges the card to sign or decrypt data.

authenticators.name: Spatio-temporal graphical password
authenticators.description: A knowledge factor where the user reproduces a visual/graphical pattern together with timing information (order and intervals) for verification.

authenticators.name: Teeth image
authenticators.description: Physiological biometric based on dental/teeth image features extracted from a photo and compared against a stored template.

authenticators.name: Temporal pattern
authenticators.description: A knowledge factor where the user reproduces a memorised timing/rhythm pattern (intervals) as the secret for verification.

authenticators.name: Textual Password
authenticators.description: A secret sequence of characters (letters, digits, symbols) chosen and memorised by the user. The user types the password and the system verifies it against a stored (hashed) value.

authenticators.name: Touch-interaction behavior
authenticators.description: Behavioral biometric based on patterns of interaction with a touch interface (timing, gestures, dynamics) captured passively and matched to a template.

authenticators.name: Unique carrier frequency offset characteristics
authenticators.description: A physical-layer fingerprint derived from a transmitter’s carrier frequency offset characteristics, measured passively and compared to reference profiles.

authenticators.name: Vertical foot acceleration patterns
authenticators.description: Behavioral biometric based on gait signatures extracted from vertical foot acceleration time-series captured passively and matched to a profile.

authenticators.name: Voice features Physiological
authenticators.description: Physiological biometric based on speaker-specific voice characteristics extracted from audio and matched to a stored voiceprint.

authenticators.name: Voice features Behavioral
authenticators.description: Behavioral biometric based on speaker-specific voice characteristics extracted from audio and matched to a stored voiceprint.
